feed.title: Compact Isogeny PQC can replace HD wallets, key-tweaking, silent payments

entry.title: Compact Isogeny PQC can replace HD wallets, key-tweaking, silent payments
entry.summary:
The recent advancement in isogeny-based cryptography has been significantly bolstered by a new research paper, which confirms the security of rerandomization techniques. Previously regarded as an unproven concept within cryptographic circles, these methods have now gained empirical backing through rigorous academic scrutiny. The validation stems from a study conducted by leading experts in the field of isogenies, marking a pivotal shift from theoretical discourse to recognized secure practice.
The implications of this validation are substantial for the development and implementation of cryptographic systems that rely on isogeny-based methods. With the confirmation of security measures, these techniques can now be more confidently applied across various digital communication infrastructures, enhancing protection against cyber threats. This breakthrough is detailed extensively in the paper, which can be accessed and reviewed [here](https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1169), providing both the methodology and results that substantiate the security claims made by the researchers.
